description: Replace the over-engineered 5-stage agentic search pipeline (9+ LLM calls, ~9 minutes) with a lean deterministic hybrid search architecture (0-2 LLM calls, <500ms-3s). Activates the currently dead supersession/relationship system. Replaces ChromaDB with sqlite-vec, rank_bm25 with SQLite FTS5, chunk-level with session-level embeddings, and adds multi-session intelligence with automatic deep mode detection.

userStories.title: Fix reasoning_docs SQLite data loss and auto-migrate existing databases
userStories.description: The reasoning_docs field is populated during indexing but never persisted to SQLite. Add the column to SCHEMA_SQL, bind it in upsert_session(), deserialize it in get_session_document(), and auto-migrate existing DBs with ALTER TABLE + a visible warning. This fixes empty fork handoffs.
acceptanceCriteria: SCHEMA_SQL includes reasoning_docs TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]' column
upsert_session() binds reasoning_docs via _serialize_list(session.reasoning_docs)
get_session_document() deserializes reasoning_docs via _deserialize_list(data.get('reasoning_docs', '[]'))
get_all_session_documents() preserves reasoning_docs through the loop
_ensure_schema() method runs on MetadataStore init: checks PRAGMA table_info, runs ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN if missing
Migration prints a visible yellow warning: 'Database migrated to schema v3 (added: reasoning_docs). Run smartfork index --rebuild to populate historical data.'
Existing data is NOT lost during migration
tests/indexer/test_metadata_store.py includes round-trip test for reasoning_docs
All existing tests continue to pass

userStories.title: Add tiered LLM configuration (strategic_llm + smart_llm)
userStories.description: Add two new config tiers so the orchestrator can use a cheap/fast model for decomposition and reranking, and a good model for judgment and synthesis. Both default to the existing llm_provider/llm_model so existing users are not broken.
acceptanceCriteria: SmartForkConfig gains strategic_llm_provider, strategic_llm_model, strategic_llm_base_url fields
SmartForkConfig gains smart_llm_provider, smart_llm_model, smart_llm_base_url fields
All new fields default to existing llm_provider, llm_model, llm_base_url respectively
config.toml round-trips the new fields correctly
save() and load() preserve the new fields
Theme validation and all other config logic unchanged
tests/cli/test_config.py or tests/test_config.py validates the new fields
mypy clean on config.py and models/config.py
ruff clean on config.py and models/config.py

userStories.title: Implement QueryDecomposer agent with structured output
userStories.description: Build a QueryDecomposer class that uses the strategic LLM to break a user query into 3-5 search sub-queries, extract entities (project, feature, tech), classify intent (status_check, task_lookup, debug, reference), and infer metadata filters. Uses complete_structured() with a Pydantic output schema.
acceptanceCriteria: QueryDecomposition Pydantic BaseModel with fields: core_goal (str), search_variants (list[str]), entities (dict), intent (str), metadata_filters (dict), prefer_recent (bool), prefer_code (bool)
QueryDecomposer class with decompose(query: str) -> QueryDecomposition method
Prompt instructs the LLM to generate 3-5 diverse search variants that capture different keyword/synonym combinations
Prompt includes examples of good vs bad sub-queries
complete_structured() call with temperature=0.1 for determinism
Graceful fallback: if LLM fails, return a QueryDecomposition with search_variants=[query] (original query only)
No hardcoded queries or results — all output generated by LLM from prompt
Unit tests with mock_llm fixture assert the prompt contains the user query
mypy clean on decomposer.py
ruff clean on decomposer.py

userStories.title: Implement ParallelRetriever that runs deterministic search across variants
userStories.description: Build a ParallelRetriever class that takes a QueryDecomposition and runs DeterministicSearchEngine.search() for each variant in parallel using ThreadPoolExecutor. Collects results, deduplicates by session_id (keeping highest deterministic score), and returns a pooled list of candidates.
acceptanceCriteria: ParallelRetriever class with retrieve(qd: QueryDecomposition, top_k: int) -> list[dict] method
Uses ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=min(5, len(qd.search_variants)))
Each variant calls deterministic_engine.search(variant, top_k=top_k * 2)
Extracts session_id, title, match_score, project_name, and full session content from each ResultCard
Deduplicates by session_id keeping the highest match_score across variants
Returns a flat list of candidate dicts with all metadata needed for reranking
Graceful fallback: if any variant fails, log warning and continue with remaining variants
If no variants produce results, return empty list
Unit tests mock DeterministicSearchEngine to verify parallel calls and deduplication
mypy clean on parallel_retriever.py
ruff clean on parallel_retriever.py

userStories.title: Implement LLM-based reranker that scores candidates against the original query
userStories.description: Build a RerankerAgent class that takes the pooled candidates and the original user query, batches candidates into groups of 5, and asks the strategic LLM to score each candidate's relevance 0.0-1.0. Uses structured output. Returns candidates re-sorted by LLM score. This bridges the semantic gap that embeddings miss.
acceptanceCriteria: RerankerAgent class with rerank(candidates: list[dict], query: str, top_n: int) -> list[dict] method
RerankResult Pydantic BaseModel with fields: session_id (str), relevance_score (float), reason (str)
BatchRerankResult Pydantic BaseModel with fields: rankings (list[RerankResult])
Processes candidates in batches of 5 to stay within token limits
Prompt includes the original query + candidate content (task_raw + summary_doc + top 3 reasoning_docs, keyword-filtered)
complete_structured() with output_schema=BatchRerankResult
Re-sorts candidates by relevance_score descending
Returns top_n candidates (default 15)
Graceful fallback: if LLM fails, return candidates sorted by deterministic match_score with a warning
Unit tests with mock_llm verify batching and sorting logic
mypy clean on reranker.py
ruff clean on reranker.py

userStories.title: Implement BatchJudgeAgent that reads full content and drops false positives
userStories.description: Build a BatchJudgeAgent class that takes top reranked candidates and the original query, reads full session content (keyword-filtered + fallback truncation), and outputs structured judgments. Scores each 0.0-1.0, flags matches_query=False for irrelevant sessions, and writes a 1-sentence reason + key_snippet quote.
acceptanceCriteria: JudgeOutput Pydantic BaseModel with fields: session_id (str), relevance_score (float), matches_query (bool), reason (str), key_snippet (str)
BatchJudgeResult Pydantic BaseModel with fields: judgments (list[JudgeOutput])
BatchJudgeAgent class with judge(candidates: list[dict], query: str, qd: QueryDecomposition) -> list[JudgeOutput] method
Smart content selection: include reasoning_docs and files_edited that contain query keywords or entity names; if fewer than 3 match, fallback to first 3 truncated docs
Prompt includes original query, core_goal, intent classification, and full candidate content
complete_structured() with output_schema=BatchJudgeResult
Drops any candidate where matches_query=False
Sorts remaining by relevance_score descending
Returns top 5 (or fewer if judge drops candidates)
Graceful fallback: if LLM fails, return all candidates with deterministic scores and empty reasons
Unit tests with mock_llm verify dropping, scoring, and sorting logic
mypy clean on judge.py
ruff clean on judge.py

userStories.title: Implement SynthesisAgent that writes query-aware excerpts and formats ResultCards
userStories.description: Build a SynthesisAgent class that takes the judged candidates and formats them into ResultCard objects. Uses the smart LLM to write query-aware excerpts (1-2 sentences answering the user's query based on key_snippet). Maps qualitative badges: Strong (>=0.8), Moderate (0.5-0.8), Weak (<0.5, dropped).
acceptanceCriteria: SynthesisAgent class with synthesize(judgments: list[JudgeOutput], candidates: list[dict], qd: QueryDecomposition) -> list[ResultCard] method
Qualitative badge mapping: >=0.8 = Strong match, 0.5-0.8 = Moderate match, <0.5 = Weak match (dropped before cards)
Badge color: green for Strong, yellow for Moderate
excerpt field uses key_snippet from JudgeOutput (not truncated summary_doc)
relevance_explanation uses reason from JudgeOutput
match_score uses relevance_score from JudgeOutput
rank assigned sequentially starting at 1
All other ResultCard fields mapped from candidate metadata (title, project_name, time_ago, duration, files_summary, tags, fork_command)
If no candidates survive judgment, return empty list with a flag so CLI can show 'No relevant sessions found'
Unit tests verify badge mapping and field population
mypy clean on synthesis.py
ruff clean on synthesis.py

userStories.title: Implement SearchOrchestrator that wires all stages into a pipeline
userStories.description: Build a SearchOrchestrator class that runs the full 5-stage pipeline: decompose -> parallel retrieve -> rerank -> judge -> synthesize. Handles live progress reporting, graceful degradation on LLM failures, and a total latency budget. Exposes a single search() method with the same signature as DeterministicSearchEngine.
acceptanceCriteria: SearchOrchestrator class with search(query: str, top_k: int = 5, project_filter: str | None = None, quality_filter: str | None = None) -> list[ResultCard] method
Pipeline stages called in order: decomposer.decompose() -> parallel_retriever.retrieve() -> reranker.rerank() -> judge.judge() -> synthesis.synthesize()
Progress reporting: prints live status lines for each stage ('Decomposing query...', 'Searching 3 variants...', 'Reranking 18 candidates...', 'Judging relevance...', 'Synthesizing results...')

LLM failure handling: if decomposer fails, use original query only; if reranker fails, use deterministic scores; if judge fails, return reranked results with warning badges; if synthesis fails, return raw cards with deterministic formatting
If all candidates are dropped by judge, return empty list
project_filter and quality_filter passed through to deterministic sub-searches
Total latency target: <15s with cloud LLM, <30s with local Ollama
Does NOT hardcode any queries, results, or session IDs — all output driven by LLM and user data
Unit tests mock all stage dependencies to verify pipeline order and error handling
mypy clean on orchestrator.py
ruff clean on orchestrator.py

userStories.title: Wire SearchOrchestrator into CLI search command with --fast fallback
userStories.description: Replace the hardcoded DeterministicSearchEngine instantiation in commands.py with SearchOrchestrator. Add a --fast flag for deterministic-only fallback. Update the search output to show qualitative badges and query-aware excerpts. Handle empty results gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: commands.py search() instantiates SearchOrchestrator by default
SearchOrchestrator receives embedder and metadata_store from CLI setup
--fast flag bypasses orchestrator and uses DeterministicSearchEngine directly (legacy path)
Result cards display: [rank] title · badge (e.g., Strong match 92%) · project_name · time_ago
Card body shows: query-aware excerpt, tags, files_summary, fork_command
Empty results: prints 'No relevant sessions found.' + the judge's reasoning for why nothing matched (if available)
Progress lines shown during orchestrator execution (decomposing, searching, reranking, judging)
Error handling: if orchestrator LLM fails entirely, show clear error + suggest --fast
Search duration displayed: '5 results · 8.4s · orchestrator (decompose + parallel + rerank + judge + synthesize)'
All existing CLI tests updated or replaced to mock SearchOrchestrator

ruff clean on commands.py

userStories.title: Build manual and synthetic test suite for the orchestrator pipeline
userStories.description: Create comprehensive tests: (A) manual test set with 10 real queries and known correct session IDs to measure recall improvement; (B) synthetic unit tests for each stage with mock LLM fixtures to prevent regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Manual test set in tests/search/test_manual_evaluation.py with at least 10 test cases
Each manual case: query (str), expected_session_id (str), description (str), minimum_expected_rank (int, default 1)
Manual test runner: runs orchestrator vs deterministic, asserts orchestrator finds expected session at or above minimum rank
Deterministic-only test asserts it FAILS to find the expected session (proves the problem exists before orchestrator)

Synthetic tests in tests/search/test_parallel_retriever.py: mock deterministic engine, assert parallel calls and deduplication
Synthetic tests in tests/search/test_reranker.py: mock LLM returns scores, assert candidates re-sorted
Synthetic tests in tests/search/test_judge.py: mock LLM returns judgments, assert false positives dropped
Synthetic tests in tests/search/test_synthesis.py: assert badge mapping and field population
Synthetic tests in tests/search/test_orchestrator.py: mock all stages, verify pipeline order and error handling
All synthetic tests pass with pytest -x
Mock LLM fixture available in conftest.py for reuse

userStories.title: Add OpenAI-compatible provider framework
userStories.description: Create a reusable OpenAICompatibleLLM class that uses the openai Python SDK (already a dependency via instructor) to call any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint. This class must implement the LLMProvider protocol (complete() and complete_structured() methods). It will be the foundation for adding 13 new providers without writing per-provider boilerplate. The class reads API keys from ~/.smartfork/secrets.env or environment variables, supports custom base_url override, and produces clear error messages when credentials are missing.
acceptanceCriteria: OpenAICompatibleLLM class exists in src/smartfork/providers/llm.py
__init__(self, model: str, api_key: str | None = None, base_url: str | None = None) reads api_key from secrets.env OR env var if not provided directly
If api_key is missing after all sources, raise RuntimeError with clear message: '<PROVIDER_NAME>_API_KEY not found. Set it in ~/.smartfork/secrets.env or as an environment variable.'
complete(prompt, max_tokens=500, temperature=0.1) -> str uses openai.OpenAI client with the configured base_url and api_key
complete_structured(prompt, output_schema, max_tokens=500, temperature=0.1) -> Any uses instructor.from_openai() to get Pydantic-validated structured output
Both methods handle exceptions gracefully: log error with loguru, raise RuntimeError with actionable message including the provider name and underlying error
No new dependencies added: relies on existing openai and instructor packages
Does NOT import provider-specific SDKs (no groq, no mistralai, no together, etc.)
Unit tests in tests/providers/test_openai_compatible.py mock the openai.OpenAI client and verify complete() returns response text
Unit tests mock instructor.from_openai() and verify complete_structured() returns a Pydantic model instance
Unit tests verify RuntimeError is raised when api_key is None and no env var is set
mypy clean on the new code
ruff clean on the new code

userStories.title: Add OpenCode ecosystem providers (opencode, go, zen)
userStories.description: Extend get_llm() factory to support three OpenCode-related providers. All three use the same OPENCODE_API_KEY and the OpenAICompatibleLLM class from US-011, but with different default base URLs. The 'go' provider is the subscription plan endpoint, 'zen' is the pay-as-you-go gateway, and 'opencode' is a user-friendly alias for go (since that's what most users expect when they say 'opencode').
acceptanceCriteria: Provider names accepted by get_llm(): 'opencode', 'go', 'zen' (case-insensitive)
opencode provider: base_url='https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1', api_key_env='OPENCODE_API_KEY', default_model='qwen3.5-plus'
go provider: base_url='https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1', api_key_env='OPENCODE_API_KEY', default_model='qwen3.5-plus'
zen provider: base_url='https://opencode.ai/zen/v1', api_key_env='OPENCODE_API_KEY', default_model='qwen3.5-plus'
All three call OpenAICompatibleLLM with their respective base_url and api_key resolved from OPENCODE_API_KEY
RuntimeError message mentions the provider name the user actually typed (e.g. 'go' or 'zen') even though they share the same env var
SmartForkConfig._backfill_tiered_llms validator recognizes 'opencode', 'go', and 'zen' as valid provider names (no-op for these, just don't reject them)
Export the three provider names or a combined helper from src/smartfork/providers/__init__.py if needed
Unit tests verify get_llm('opencode') returns an OpenAICompatibleLLM with correct base_url
Unit tests verify get_llm('go') and get_llm('zen') have distinct base_urls
mypy clean
ruff clean

userStories.title: Add 10 popular third-party OpenAI-compatible providers
userStories.description: Extend get_llm() factory to support 10 additional popular LLM API providers. All use OpenAICompatibleLLM from US-011 with provider-specific defaults for base_url, env var, and model. This gives users maximum flexibility: free tiers (OpenRouter, Groq, Gemini), cheap pay-as-you-go (Together, DeepSeek), and premium options (xAI, Perplexity).
acceptanceCriteria: Provider names accepted by get_llm() (case-insensitive): 'openrouter', 'groq', 'gemini', 'together', 'mistral', 'deepseek', 'fireworks', 'cohere', 'xai', 'perplexity'

groq: base_url='https://api.groq.com/openai/v1', env='GROQ_API_KEY', default='llama-3.3-70b-versatile'

mistral: base_url='https://api.mistral.ai/v1', env='MISTRAL_API_KEY', default='mistral-large-latest'
deepseek: base_url='https://api.deepseek.com', env='DEEPSEEK_API_KEY', default='deepseek-chat'
fireworks: base_url='https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1', env='FIREWORKS_API_KEY', default='accounts/fireworks/models/llama-v3p1-8b-instruct'
cohere: base_url='https://api.cohere.com/v1', env='COHERE_API_KEY', default='command-r'
xai: base_url='https://api.x.ai/v1', env='XAI_API_KEY', default='grok-2-latest'
perplexity: base_url='https://api.perplexity.ai', env='PERPLEXITY_API_KEY', default='sonar'
SmartForkConfig._backfill_tiered_llms validator recognizes all 10 new names as valid provider names
README or AGENTS.md updated with a provider table showing: provider name, env var, example model, notes (free tier?)
Unit tests verify each provider name maps to correct base_url and default model via get_llm()
Unit tests use monkeypatch to set env vars and verify get_llm() instantiates OpenAICompatibleLLM correctly

userStories.title: Create SessionRelationship, Chain, Timeline models and QueryInterpretation structured output model
userStories.description: Create the foundational data models for the new architecture. SessionRelationship replaces the flat SupersessionLink with support for continuation/supersession/branch/related types. QueryInterpretation is the structured output from the single-LLM-call QueryInterpreter. Timeline models support multi-session display. All models include validation rules and are exported from models/__init__.py.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionRelationship dataclass with fields: from_session, to_session, relationship_type, confidence, detected_by, reasons, created_at
relationship_type restricted to: 'continuation', 'supersession', 'branch', 'related'
detected_by restricted to: 'explicit', 'heuristic', 'llm_verified'
confidence range validation: 0.0 to 1.0
Chain dataclass with fields: sessions, chain_type, head_session_id, tail_session_id
TimelineEntry dataclass with fields: session_id, timestamp, task, quality_tag, summary, relationship_to_next
TimelineSummary dataclass with fields: narrative, timeline, suggested_fork_session_id, resolution_status
QueryInterpretation dataclass with fields: original_query, keywords, synonyms, expanded_query, temporal_filter, intent, project_hint, multi_session_confidence, needs_deep_mode, quality_boost
SessionEnrichment dataclass with fields: title, summary, quality_tag, tech_tags
Add parent_id and previous_session_id fields to RawSessionData and SessionDocument
All models exported from models/__init__.py
Validation tests in tests/models/test_relationship.py, test_query.py, test_enrichment.py

userStories.title: Define complete SQLite schema with sessions_fts (FTS5), session_vectors (sqlite-vec), and session_relationships tables
userStories.description: Replace the existing schema with a comprehensive schema that supports all new features. Add FTS5 virtual table for full-text search, sqlite-vec virtual table for embeddings, and session_relationships table for the graph. Include triggers to keep FTS5 in sync. Implement v3-to-v4 migration that preserves existing data and migrates supersession_links to session_relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: SCHEMA_SQL includes sessions table (v4, schema_version=4)
session_relationships table with PK(from_session, to_session, relationship_type), FKs to sessions, indexes
supersession_links table kept for backward compatibility (deprecated)
sessions_fts virtual table using fts5(tokenize='porter unicode61')
session_vectors virtual table using vec0(session_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, embedding float[1024])
FTS5 triggers: sessions_fts_insert, sessions_fts_update, sessions_fts_delete (auto-sync with sessions table)
WAL mode enabled (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL)
Foreign keys enabled (PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON)
Schema migration: _ensure_schema() detects v3, runs _migrate_to_v4()
Migration copies supersession_links to session_relationships with type='supersession'
Migration populates sessions_fts from existing sessions data
Migration creates session_vectors table (empty, will be filled on re-index)
MetadataStore CRUD: add_relationship(), get_relationships(), get_relationships_by_type(), delete_relationships_for_session()
MetadataStore search: fts5_search() returns list[(session_id, bm25_score)]
MetadataStore vector: store_vector(), get_vector(), vector_search(), delete_vector()

userStories.title: Fix OpenCode adapter to preserve parent_id from SQLite session table

Fix the adapter to pass parent_id through to RawSessionData, and update SessionParser to propagate it to SessionDocument. This enables explicit relationship detection for OpenCode sessions.

Test: ClaudeCode adapter (no parent_id) still works, produces SessionDocument with parent_id=None

userStories.title: Build SessionEssenceBuilder to create condensed session text for embedding
userStories.description: Create a SessionEssenceBuilder that composes a ~500-5000 token text from task_raw, summary_doc, and top 3 reasoning_docs. This replaces chunk-level embedding with session-level embedding, preserving the full reasoning arc while fitting in the embedding model context window. Supports instruction-aware embedding prefixes for Ollama.

Essence includes: Task, Summary, Reasoning 1-3, Technologies
Each reasoning block truncated to max_reasoning_length (default 1500 chars)

userStories.title: Rewrite EmbeddingPipeline to store session-level vectors in sqlite-vec instead of chunk-level in ChromaDB

userStories.title: Build RelationshipDetector with explicit, heuristic, and optional LLM-verified tiers
userStories.description: Create a RelationshipDetector that detects session relationships at index time. Tier 1 uses explicit parent_id from OpenCode. Tier 2 uses heuristics (temporal proximity, file overlap, embedding similarity, workspace match). Tier 3 (optional, disabled by default) uses LLM verification for ambiguous cases. Optimized O(n log n) algorithm groups by project and compares forward-only.
acceptanceCriteria: RelationshipDetector class with detect_all(sessions, vectors) -> list[SessionRelationship]
_detect_explicit(): uses parent_id, creates 'continuation' relationships with confidence=1.0
_detect_heuristic(): groups by project, sorts by time, compares forward-only
Hard filters: same session skip, >30 day gap skip, no domain overlap skip
Scoring: temporal (<24h) +0.30, file overlap (>30%) +0.25, embedding sim (>0.8) +0.20, workspace +0.15, resolution boost +0.10
_classify_relationship(): returns 'supersession', 'continuation', 'branch', or 'related' based on metadata
_is_resolved(): checks quality_tag == 'solution_found'
_significant_task_overlap(): compares task vocabulary overlap >50%
_cosine_similarity(): computes cosine sim between two vectors
Test: explicit detection creates relationship from parent_id
Test: heuristic detection finds temporal + file overlap sessions
Test: forward-only optimization (no reverse comparisons)
Test: hard filters exclude cross-project and >30 day gap pairs

userStories.title: Fix SupersessionDetector O(n²) and no-vector threshold bugs
userStories.description: Optimize SupersessionDetector.find_supersessions() from O(n²) over all permutations to O(n log n) sort + O(n²/2) forward-only comparisons. Fix the bug where detect_supersession without vectors maxes at 0.65 score but threshold is 0.85, making it a silent no-op. Increase base score when vectors are absent or make vectors mandatory.
acceptanceCriteria: find_supersessions(): groups by project, sorts by time, compares only i < j (forward)
Removes unnecessary time guard in detect_supersession (forward-only guarantees newer > older)
Fix no-vector bug: increase base score from 0.5 to 0.6 when vectors absent
OR: make vectors mandatory and skip supersession detection if no vectors
Increase resolution boosts: unresolved older +0.15 (was +0.10), resolved newer +0.10 (was +0.05)
Test: 300 sessions index in <5 minutes (including relationship detection)
Test: forward-only reduces comparisons by ~50% vs all permutations
Test: no-vector detection now triggers with score >= 0.85 threshold
mypy clean on supersession.py
ruff clean on supersession.py

userStories.title: Update FullIndexer to use session-level embedding and add relationship detection phase
userStories.description: Modify FullIndexer to replace chunk-level embedding with session-level embedding using SessionEssenceBuilder and the rewritten EmbeddingPipeline. Add relationship detection after all sessions for a project are stored. Wire the new components together in the correct order.
acceptanceCriteria: FullIndexer removes chunker.chunk() call entirely
FullIndexer calls SessionEssenceBuilder before embedding
FullIndexer calls embedder.embed_session() instead of embed_and_store(chunks)
After all sessions stored, FullIndexer runs RelationshipDetector per project
RelationshipDetector receives session vectors for similarity scoring
Detected relationships stored via MetadataStore.add_relationship()
Progress reporting updated: shows 'detecting relationships' phase
Stats updated: includes relationships count
Test: index_all produces 1 vector per session (not 5 chunks)
Test: index_all creates relationships for sessions with parent_id
Test: index_all creates heuristic relationships for related sessions
mypy clean on indexer.py
ruff clean on indexer.py

userStories.title: Build QueryInterpreter with multi-session confidence detection
userStories.description: Create a QueryInterpreter that replaces the 5-stage decomposer with a single structured LLM call. The interpreter extracts keywords, expands synonyms, detects intent, and computes multi_session_confidence (0.0-1.0) to automatically route queries to single-session or multi-session search paths. Supports explicit --fast and --deep flags.
acceptanceCriteria: QueryInterpreter class with interpret(query: str) -> QueryInterpretation
1 structured LLM call returns: keywords, synonyms, intent, multi_session_confidence, project_hint
Synonym expansion using hardcoded dictionary: auth->authentication/login/oauth/jwt, bug->error/fix/issue/crash, db->database/postgresql/mysql, etc.
Intent detection: solution_hunting, error_recall, continuation, temporal_lookup, decision_hunting, implementation_lookup, vague_memory
multi_session_confidence rules: temporal words (>0.75), continuation words (>0.80), specific keywords (<0.40)
needs_deep_mode: True if confidence > 0.75 OR --deep flag passed
Fallback: if LLM fails, use deterministic keyword extraction + synonym dict + intent patterns
Test: 'jwt auth bug' -> confidence < 0.40, needs_deep_mode=False
Test: 'what happened with auth across sessions' -> confidence > 0.75, needs_deep_mode=True
Test: 'continue my API work' -> confidence > 0.80, needs_deep_mode=True
Test: LLM failure falls back to deterministic parsing
mypy clean on query_interpreter.py
ruff clean on query_interpreter.py

userStories.title: Rewrite DeterministicSearchEngine with FTS5 keyword search and sqlite-vec vector search
userStories.description: Replace the broken per-query BM25 rebuild with persistent SQLite FTS5. Replace ChromaDB vector search with sqlite-vec. Keep RRF fusion. Fix missing reranker signals (file overlap 20%, keyword density 15%). Add relationship-based scoring (latest in chain +15%, superseded -30%).
acceptanceCriteria: _bm25_search() replaced with MetadataStore.fts5_search() (single SQL query, no rebuild)
_vector_search() replaced with MetadataStore.vector_search() (sqlite-vec KNN)
_rrf_fuse() kept with same logic, different input sources
_rerank() adds file_overlap scoring (20% weight) and keyword_density scoring (15% weight)
_rerank() adds relationship boost: latest_in_chain +15%, superseded -30%
Synonym expansion: query expanded using dictionary before FTS5 search
FTS5 query format: (auth OR authentication OR login OR jwt) AND (bug OR error OR fix)
Test: FTS5 search returns results in <50ms for 300 sessions
Test: vector search returns results using sqlite-vec
Test: RRF fusion combines FTS5 + vector ranks correctly
Test: relationship boost promotes latest sessions and demotes superseded ones
mypy clean on deterministic.py
ruff clean on deterministic.py

userStories.title: Rewrite SearchOrchestrator to use QueryInterpreter + DeterministicSearchEngine with optional MultiSessionSynthesizer
userStories.description: Replace the 5-stage pipeline (decompose→retrieve→rerank→judge→synthesize) with the new architecture: QueryInterpreter (1 call) → DeterministicSearchEngine (0 calls) → optional MultiSessionSynthesizer (1 call for deep mode). Remove RerankerAgent and BatchJudgeAgent from the default path. Keep SynthesisAgent for ResultCard formatting.
acceptanceCriteria: SearchOrchestrator.search() calls: QueryInterpreter.interpret() -> DeterministicSearchEngine.search() -> SessionGraphEngine (if deep) -> MultiSessionSynthesizer (if deep)
Default mode: 1 LLM call (QueryInterpreter) + deterministic search
Deep mode: 2 LLM calls (QueryInterpreter + MultiSessionSynthesizer)
Fast mode (--fast): 0 LLM calls, skip QueryInterpreter entirely
Remove RerankerAgent and BatchJudgeAgent from default path (keep in legacy/ if needed)
Progress reporting: shows interpreter, search, graph, synthesis phases
LLM failure handling: if interpreter fails, use deterministic parsing; if synthesizer fails, return ResultCards without narrative
project_filter and quality_filter passed through to DeterministicSearchEngine
Total latency: <3s default, <500ms fast, <4s deep
Test: default mode uses 1 LLM call
Test: deep mode uses 2 LLM calls
Test: fast mode uses 0 LLM calls
Test: all failure modes have graceful fallbacks
mypy clean on orchestrator.py
ruff clean on orchestrator.py

userStories.title: Build SessionGraphEngine for chain following and relationship traversal
userStories.description: Create a SessionGraphEngine that queries the session_relationships table to follow continuation chains, group search results by chains, find the latest session in a chain, detect branches, and build structured timelines. This is the core multi-session intelligence component.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionGraphEngine class with methods: find_chain(), find_related_sessions(), get_latest_in_chain(), find_branches(), build_timeline()
find_chain(session_id, direction='forward') -> list[SessionDocument]: follows continuation links
find_related_sessions(query, project=None, max_chains=5) -> list[Chain]: groups by chains
get_latest_in_chain(chain) -> SessionDocument: returns rightmost (most recent)
find_branches(session_id) -> list[list[SessionDocument]]: all divergent paths
build_timeline(chain) -> list[TimelineEntry]: structured timeline entries
Uses MetadataStore.get_relationships() for graph queries
Caches chain lookups to avoid repeated DB queries
Test: chain following returns ordered sessions [A->B->C]
Test: branch detection finds parallel paths from common parent
Test: timeline includes dates, tasks, quality, resolution status
Test: latest_in_chain returns most recent session
mypy clean on session_graph_engine.py
ruff clean on session_graph_engine.py

userStories.title: Build MultiSessionSynthesizer for timeline and narrative generation
userStories.description: Create a MultiSessionSynthesizer that takes a query and grouped session chains, then uses 1 structured LLM call to generate a human-readable timeline, narrative summary, resolution status, and fork suggestion. This is the 'deep mode' output that differentiates multi-session queries from simple search.
acceptanceCriteria: MultiSessionSynthesizer class with synthesize_timeline(query, chains) -> TimelineSummary
1 structured LLM call per deep mode search
Input: user query + list of Chain objects (each with sessions and metadata)
Output: TimelineSummary { narrative, timeline, suggested_fork_session_id, resolution_status }
Prompt includes: query, chain entries with dates/tasks/quality/summaries
narrative: human-readable paragraph summarizing the journey
timeline: structured entries with session_id, timestamp, task, quality_tag, summary, relationship_to_next
suggested_fork_session_id: latest session in primary chain
resolution_status: 'solved' | 'ongoing' | 'dead_end' | 'mixed'
Fallback: if LLM fails, return raw chain info without narrative
Test: synthesize produces non-empty narrative for valid chains
Test: synthesize returns fallback for empty chains
Test: LLM failure returns basic chain listing
mypy clean on multi_session_synthesizer.py
ruff clean on multi_session_synthesizer.py

userStories.title: Wire supersession/relationship awareness into search results and fork command
userStories.description: After retrieving search results, query session_relationships to annotate ResultCards with supersession indicators (⬆ superseded, ✅ latest). Boost/demote scores based on relationship status. In the fork command, check if target session is superseded and warn user with suggestion to fork from latest.
acceptanceCriteria: Search results query MetadataStore.get_relationships() for each candidate
ResultCard.supersession_note populated: '⬆ Superseded by X' or '✅ Latest in chain'
Superseded sessions: match_score *= 0.7 (demote 30%)
Latest in chain sessions: match_score += 0.15 (boost 15%)
Fork command checks get_superseding_sessions(session_id) before assembly
If superseded: pass supersession_warning to ForkAssembler.assemble()
CLI displays warning: 'This session was superseded by X. Consider forking from the latest version.'
Fix ForkAssembler._llm_assemble() to include supersession_warning in LLM prompt
Test: search results show supersession annotations
Test: superseded sessions ranked lower than latest
Test: fork from superseded session shows warning
Test: fork from latest session shows no warning
mypy clean on deterministic.py, commands.py, assembler.py
ruff clean on deterministic.py, commands.py, assembler.py

userStories.title: Update CLI search display with --fast, --deep, --mode flags and supersession annotations
userStories.description: Update the CLI search command to support the three search modes (fast, default, deep). Render ResultCard.supersession_note in the display. Show timeline panel in deep mode. Add search mode indicator in output footer.
acceptanceCriteria: --fast flag: skip QueryInterpreter, use DeterministicSearchEngine directly
--deep flag: force multi-session mode regardless of confidence
--mode flag: --mode=deterministic | --mode=deep (explicit mode selection)
Default (no flags): auto-detect via multi_session_confidence
Result cards show supersession indicator: ⬆ or ✅ emoji with note text
Deep mode output: ResultCards + Timeline panel + Narrative section
Confidence 0.40-0.75: show suggestion '3 related sessions found. Add --deep for timeline.'
Search duration and mode displayed in footer: '5 results · 2.3s · default mode'
Empty results: show 'No sessions found' with helpful suggestions

Test: --deep triggers MultiSessionSynthesizer
Test: auto-detect correctly routes queries
Test: supersession annotations rendered in display

Test: no rank_bm25 imports remain in active code

userStories.title: Fix config precedence so environment variables override TOML values
userStories.description: The current config system passes all TOML values as init kwargs to SmartForkConfig(), which causes them to override environment variables. Fix settings_customise_sources() (or add it) so the pydantic-settings source order is: init kwargs (empty) > env vars > .env file > TOML file > defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement settings_customise_sources() in SmartForkConfig
Source order: env_prefix (SMARTFORK_*) > .env file > config.toml > field defaults
TOML values are read but NOT passed as init kwargs (prevent override of env vars)
If a field is set via TOML and also via env var, env var wins
Test: SMARTFORK_LLM_MODEL=gpt-4 overrides config.toml llm_model=qwen2.5-coder:7b
Test: unset env var falls back to TOML value
Test: missing TOML value falls back to field default
mypy clean on config.py
ruff clean on config.py

userStories.title: Wire SearchCache into DeterministicSearchEngine
userStories.description: The SearchCache class exists but is never instantiated or used. Wire it into DeterministicSearchEngine so repeated identical queries return cached results. Cache key: hash of query + project_filter + quality_filter + top_k. Support cache invalidation when sessions are re-indexed.
acceptanceCriteria: DeterministicSearchEngine initializes SearchCache in __init__
search() checks cache before executing query
Cache hit: returns cached ResultCard list directly
Cache miss: executes search, stores results in cache
Cache key: hash of 'query|project_filter|quality_filter|top_k|search_mode'
Cache TTL: 300 seconds (configurable)
Cache invalidation: MetadataStore.upsert_session() triggers cache.invalidate_session()
Test: identical query returns cached results on second call
Test: cache respects TTL (expires after 300s)
Test: new indexing invalidates affected cache entries
mypy clean on cache.py and deterministic.py
ruff clean on cache.py and deterministic.py

userStories.title: Build comprehensive test suite for new architecture components
userStories.description: Create unit and integration tests for all new components: QueryInterpreter, DeterministicSearchEngine (FTS5+vec), SessionGraphEngine, MultiSessionSynthesizer, RelationshipDetector, SessionEssenceBuilder. Use mock LLM fixtures. Test the three search paths (fast/default/deep). Test error handling and fallback paths.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/search/test_query_interpreter.py: mock LLM, assert structured output parsing
tests/search/test_deterministic_engine.py: FTS5 search, vector search, RRF fusion, reranking with relationship boosts
tests/search/test_session_graph_engine.py: chain following, branch detection, timeline building
tests/search/test_multi_session_synthesizer.py: mock LLM, assert TimelineSummary structure
tests/indexer/test_session_essence_builder.py: essence composition, truncation, instruction prefix
tests/indexer/test_relationship_detector.py: explicit detection, heuristic scoring, classification
tests/search/test_integration.py: end-to-end search for all three modes (fast/default/deep)
tests/search/test_fallbacks.py: LLM unavailable, vector DB empty, FTS5 empty, no relationships
Mock LLM fixture in conftest.py that supports complete() and complete_structured()
Mock MetadataStore fixture with in-memory SQLite for fast tests
All tests pass with pytest -x

userStories.title: Manual evaluation: verify search quality improvements with real queries
userStories.description: Run manual evaluation tests with 10-20 real-world queries against the user's actual session database. Compare old 5-stage pipeline vs new architecture. Measure: latency, LLM calls, relevance (expected session in top 3), and multi-session accuracy (correct chain detection).
acceptanceCriteria: 10-20 manual test cases with expected results
Queries: specific ('jwt auth bug'), vague ('that database thing'), temporal ('last week'), multi-session ('what happened with auth')
Measure latency per query: fast mode <500ms, default <3s, deep <4s
Measure LLM calls per query: fast 0, default 1, deep 2
Expected session found in top 3 for >=80% of specific queries
Multi-session chains correctly grouped for >=70% of timeline queries
Supersession annotations accurate (verified by human inspection)
Document results in tests/search/MANUAL_EVALUATION.md
No regressions: all existing tests still pass
